summary: First-iteration E2E review of HRMS (Lumen HR). Backend: 37 pytest tests run, 35 passed (94.6%). Frontend: verified admin + employee flows, login form, role-based redirects, sidebar filtering, leave/attendance pages. One CRITICAL backend RBAC bug found on dashboard routes; one frontend design issue (native date pickers).

critical.issue: Missing role check — any authenticated user (e.g. employee1@hrms.com) can fetch admin dashboard data (total_employees, headcount_by_department, attendance_trend, pending_leaves). Route uses get_current_user only; needs require_roles(['management_admin']).
Same RBAC miss — any authenticated user can access manager dashboard (returns empty team for non-managers but is reachable). Needs require_roles(['senior_manager','management_admin']).

minor.issue: Observed one transient 502 during navigation; not reproducible. Likely worker hiccup. Monitor.

issues: Start Date and End Date inputs use native browser date pickers (input[type=date] showing mm/dd/yyyy) instead of the shadcn Calendar/Popover component. Per design guidelines, replace with shadcn calendar.

action_items: Add role-based access control to /api/v1/dashboard/admin (management_admin only) — currently any authenticated user including employees can read full admin counts and trend data. This is a real privilege-escalation/data-leak.
Add role-based access control to /api/v1/dashboard/manager (senior_manager, management_admin).
Replace native HTML date inputs in Leave Apply form (and any other date inputs) with shadcn Calendar component for visual consistency.
Optional: investigate the transient 502 on /api/v1/dashboard/admin (single occurrence during nav).
critical_code_review_comments: app/api/routes/dashboard.py: admin_dashboard and manager_dashboard only depend on get_current_user. They should use require_roles dependency similar to other admin-only endpoints (employees POST works correctly with admin guard).
Clock-out endpoint returns 409 'Already clocked out' which is correct behavior; tests should reset state between runs.
Login response correctly returns access_token, refresh_token, role, full_name, user_id for all 4 demo accounts. JWT format valid.
Frontend sidebar correctly filters by role (admin: Dashboard/Employees/Departments/Attendance/Leave Approvals/Settings; employee: Dashboard/Attendance/Leave/Payslips/Performance).
Protected route guards work: no-token /admin → /login, wrong-role /admin as employee → /employee.
Admin dashboard renders 4 metric cards + line chart + bar chart (12 SVG elements detected).
Employee list page: 9 rows + search + dept filter + pagination present.
Today attendance page (admin): renders 'Who's in today' table with code/name/dept/clock-in/clock-out/status.
test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md — all 4 demo accounts (admin/manager/recruiter/employee1 @ hrms.com) verified working.
seed_data_creation: Did not need to re-run seed. Created a few TEST_ prefixed records via API tests (one TEST employee EMP code TEST25B834, one TEST department, several TEST leave applications). Cleanup not implemented — main agent may wish to add prefix-based cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Two failing tests: (1) test_clock_out_employee — flaky due to test state (employee2 already clocked out today), benign; (2) test_admin_dashboard_forbidden_for_employee — REAL BUG, will fail until RBAC is added to dashboard routes. Frontend public URL: https://hr-dashboard-227.preview.emergentagent.com. Notification bell loads correctly. Manager team attendance, leave approval, employee CRUD, clock-in/out 409 conflict, leave apply validation all verified.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA for dashboard RBAC miss: app/api/routes/dashboard.py declares admin_dashboard with `_: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)]` — the underscore name signals the user object is intentionally unused, but there is no `require_roles(['management_admin'])` dependency. Reproduction: login as employee1@hrms.com -> curl GET /api/v1/dashboard/admin with bearer token -> returns 200 with full org-wide counts (verified: total_employees=9, headcount_by_department, 6-month attendance_trend). Mitigation: replace dependency with `_: User = Depends(require_roles(['management_admin']))` similar to admin-only POST /employees route. Same fix needed for /dashboard/manager (limit to senior_manager + management_admin).
